stem: A company's cost C (in dollars) to produce n widgets is given by C = 800 + 5n, and the revenue R from selling n widgets is R = 12n. The company wants the profit to be at least 30% of the cost. What is the minimum number of widgets the company must produce and sell to achieve this goal?
explanation: The profit is R - C = 12n - (800 + 5n) = 7n - 800. We need the profit to be at least 30% of the cost: 7n - 800 ≥ 0.30(800 + 5n). Expanding the right side: 7n - 800 ≥ 240 + 1.5n. Subtracting 1.5n from both sides: 5.5n - 800 ≥ 240. Adding 800: 5.5n ≥ 1040. Dividing by 5.5: n ≥ 189.09. Since n must be a whole number, the minimum is n = 190. Verification: C = 800 + 5(190) = 1750, R = 12(190) = 2280, profit = 530, and 530/1750 ≈ 0.303 = 30.3% ≥ 30%.

distractorRationale.A: Solved 7n - 800 = 0.30(800) without including the variable cost component. 7n - 800 = 240, so 7n = 1040, giving n = 148.57, rounded incorrectly to 160.
distractorRationale.B: Set profit equal to 30% of revenue instead of cost. 7n - 800 = 0.30(12n) gives 7n - 800 = 3.6n, so 3.4n = 800, giving n = 235.29, but made arithmetic error = 171.
distractorRationale.C: Found n ≥ 189.09 correctly but rounded down instead of up to the nearest integer = 189.
distractorRationale.D: Correct answer: properly set up inequality, solved to get n ≥ 189.09, and rounded up to n = 190.
